items.title: Melbourne teacher charged after camera found in school bathroom

children.content: The 33-year-old was arrested at the school in the city's south-east after the discovery of a camera in a bathroom.

items.title: On-duty paramedic stabbed outside cafe in Melbourne's north

children.content: Police have arrested a man who allegedly stabbed an on-duty paramedic in the suburb of Reservoir, in Melbourne's north, before fleeing the scene.

items.title: Wine code of conduct details emerge as industry continues to shrink

children.content: Industry representatives welcome a new mandatory code of conduct that dictates how wineries treat wine grape growers, but it may be too late for some producers.

image.altText: Rows of grapevines lie in darkness as the yellow sun peeks over distant hills

items.title: 'Free Palestine' party plans to funnel votes to Victorian conservatives

children.content: Victorian voters are being urged to look out for "misleading" party names, as right-wing activists seek to redirect progressive votes to conservative parties.

image.altText: Avi Yemeni

items.title: Roadworks on hold as regional councils grapple with fuel prices

children.content: Regional roadworks and construction projects are being put on hold as Victorian councils tighten their budgets amid the international fuel crisis.

image.altText: A road sign of a rough road

items.title: Childcare group G8 to close 10pc of centres after child abuse scandal

children.content: About 36,000 children are enrolled under the brand's 395 childcare and early learning centres, which include centres such as Headstart, Jellybeans, Kool Kids and Kindy Patch Kids.

items.title: Melbourne rush-hour chaos as hundreds of rail passengers left stranded

children.content: A rail equipment fault caused havoc on the V/Line Geelong Line on Wednesday night, with rail operators warning of more delays today.

image.altText: Jūn Háo Bái 2

items.title: New study suggest Australians largely support renewable transition

children.content: A new research study indicates a majority of regional Australians support the renewable transition despite some protests over planned infrastructure.

image.altText: Wind turbines over the rolling hills.

items.title: Three Melbourne teens charged over alleged attempted kidnapping

children.content: Police say the trio are linked to targeted attacks on promoters of a weekly event held in South Melbourne.

image.altText: Police tape at a crime scene

items.title: Victorian opposition vows $20m saving with public servant salary cap

children.content: The Coalition is promising to cap the salaries of senior public sector executives at just under $600,000 a year to "restore financial discipline".

image.altText: A man in a navy jacket, white shirt and light blue tie stands behind a blonde woman in a lilac jacket and white top.

items.title: Hundreds of child abuse files found on police killer Dezi Freeman's devices

children.content: Police killer Dezi Freeman was on the verge of having his secret stash of child abuse material discovered when he gunned down two officers outside his makeshift home.

items.title: AFL pushes ahead with draft changes and not everyone is happy

items.title: Tigers, Eagles or Cockle Divers — what makes up a football club's name?

children.content: How have Australia's football clubs come to be named, and what do those names represent? We analysed over 1,500 senior Aussie Rules clubs to find the patterns in how clubs have named themselves.

image.altText: Local footy

items.title: Melbourne-to-Queensland exodus in 'reversal' amid housing unaffordability

children.content: South-East Queensland has been under-represented in 5% Deposit Scheme approvals, with prospective first-home buyers heading to Melbourne for its "considerable affordability" benefits.

items.title: Farrer voters speak up as pre-polling begins in blockbuster by-election

children.content: Pre-polling commences in the seat of Farrer for the hotly contested by-election triggered by the resignation of former opposition leader Sussan Ley.

image.altText: People in coloured t-shirts congregate on the seat outside a building.

items.title: VicGrid surveyors cancel access visit after farmgate protest makes them feel unsafe

children.content: Renewable planning body VicGrid has threatened a high-profile anti-transmission-line campaigner with enforcement action after government surveyors felt "hindered" from accessing his farm.

items.title: Flirtation turns to something much darker in show set at Australian Open

children.content: What starts as a rom-com set at the Australian Open turns into a searing indictment of race relations in Australia in the new show Game. Set. Match.

image.altText: Megan Wilding, an Indigenous woman in her mid-30s, pokes her head through a plastic curtain, looking determined.

items.title: How self-employment can help autistic people avoid workplace challenges

children.content: After the National Autism Strategy identifies the potential of self-employment to provide meaningful work for people with autism, neurodivergent people who are already self-employed say it can help overcome workplace barriers.

items.title: Telehealth ban meant Sue died before she could access assisted dying

items.title: Council passes vote of no confidence in mayor over 'morally reprehensible' character reference

children.content: Wyndham councillors in Melbourne's south-west unanimously pass a vote of no confidence in Mayor Preet Singh over a character reference he provided to a convicted child sex offender.

items.title: Man who randomly killed British tourist not criminally responsible

items.title: Weapons maker Thales loses out on multi-billion-dollar missile contract

children.content: Thales was the favourite to win the contract to develop a mobile missile system for Australia, but now the future of its StrikeMaster missile launcher is uncertain.

image.altText: A StrikeMaster vehicle.

items.title: AI is helping rangers protect Victorian wildlife in a fraction of the time

children.content: Researchers say the dolphin appears healthy and is most likely chasing prey in the Melbourne waterway.

items.title: 'What crime did we commit?': Indigenous leaders call for respect after Anzac booing

articles.title: 'Carry the hope': Thousands gather for Anzac Day service in Canberra
